food_subgroup: Milk tea / bubble tea / boba beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic milk tea entry (FOODON_03311737). The Alley Deer Papa is a branded brown sugar variant with tapioca pearls; closest_parent used as no exact branded entry exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0GKM covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific brown sugar milk tea or bubble tea code exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for branded bubble tea / brown sugar milk tea products.

preparation_method: served chilled or at room temperature
allergens: may contain gluten (tapioca pearls may be processed in shared facilities)

serving.description: 1 large cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g as served)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard large cup of The Alley Deer Papa Brown Sugar Milk Tea, approximately 500 ml, as typically served at The Alley outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Bubble tea (milk tea with tapioca pearls, brown sugar) — nutrient composition estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for milk tea and tapioca pearls, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient data for sweetened milk tea beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact branded entry for The Alley Deer Papa exists in any approved nutrient database. Values are estimated by combining: (1) HPB Singapore data for sweetened milk tea (approximately 60–70 kcal/100 ml for standard milk tea), (2) USDA FoodData Central data for tapioca pearls (cooked, ~130 kcal/100 g), and (3) brown sugar syrup contribution. The final blended estimate for the ready-to-drink beverage as served (with pearls distributed) is approximately 80–90 kcal/100 g. A midpoint of 85 kcal/100 g is used. Protein from milk (~1.5 g/100 g), fat from milk (~1.5 g/100 g), carbohydrate predominantly from brown sugar and tapioca (~16 g/100 g). These are conservative estimates; actual values depend on The Alley's proprietary recipe and portion ratios.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened milk tea and tapioca pearl beverages — analogous estimate based on GI values for sweetened milk tea (~55–65) and cooked tapioca pearls (~70) from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for The Alley Deer Papa Brown Sugar Milk Tea. GI estimated at 62 based on: (1) University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened milk tea (GI ~55–65, medium category); (2) cooked tapioca pearls GI ~70 (high); (3) brown sugar GI ~65. Weighted blended estimate for the ready-to-drink beverage with pearls yields approximately GI 62 (medium). Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (62 × 80 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 49.6. Available carbohydrate per 500 g serving = 16 g/100 g × 500 g = 80 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~25 mg/100 g); primary concern is added sugar contributing to weight gain which indirectly elevates blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: The Alley Deer Papa is low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertensive risk, but its high added sugar content (~12.5 g/100 g; ~62.5 g per 500 ml serving) may contribute to weight gain and metabolic dysfunction that indirectly raises blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to occasional treats.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from brown sugar syrup and tapioca pearls; medium-to-high glycemic load per serving (~49.6 GL units per 500 ml cup)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise significant caution with this beverage: a single 500 ml serving delivers approximately 80 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~49.6, which can cause substantial postprandial blood glucose elevation; consumption should be infrequent, portion-reduced, or substituted with a sugar-free or reduced-sugar variant if available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk; moderate saturated fat from dairy milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of high-sugar beverages such as this brown sugar milk tea is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with existing cardiovascular disease should limit intake to no more than occasional consumption and consider requesting reduced-sugar preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Alley Reindeer Plushie Deer Papa (鹿爸爸黑糖奶茶) is a signature brown sugar milk tea beverage sold by The Alley (小鹿茶), a Taiwanese-origin specialty tea brand with outlets in Singapore including The Alley at Funan Mall. The drink is characterised by its rich brown sugar syrup swirled through fresh milk and brewed black tea, served with chewy tapioca pearls (boba). The beverage is sweet, creamy, and mildly tea-flavoured, with a distinctive caramel-like depth from the brown sugar. The tapioca pearls add a soft, chewy textural contrast. As a ready-to-drink sweetened milk tea, it is classified as a processed beverage. Nutritionally, a standard 500 ml serving delivers approximately 425 kcal, 80 g of carbohydrate (predominantly from brown sugar and tapioca), 7.5 g of protein from milk, and 7.5 g of fat. The glycemic load per serving is high (~49.6), making it a significant source of rapidly absorbed carbohydrates. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, or cardiovascular disease. The product is halal-certified and vegetarian-friendly. It is not vegan due to the use of fresh dairy milk. The Alley is known for its premium positioning in the Taiwanese milk tea segment and its distinctive deer mascot branding.

confidence_reason: No exact branded nutritional data for The Alley Deer Papa exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from approved reference data for sweetened milk tea and tapioca pearls. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is sound and based on approved sources, but actual values depend on The Alley's proprietary recipe.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from approved sources (HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central) as no exact branded entry exists for The Alley Deer Papa. Actual nutritional content will vary based on The Alley's proprietary recipe, cup size, and customisation options (sugar level, ice level, pearl quantity). 2. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 as this beverage contains no significant vitamin C; confirmed zero per reference data for milk tea beverages — naturally zero for this beverage type. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used for this estimation. 4. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 5. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 6. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 12. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 13. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources. 16. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a milk-tea beverage with no partially hydrogenated oils; naturally zero by product composition. 17. GI set to 62 (medium) as an analogous estimate; glycemic load per 500 g serving = 49.6. 18. The food_name has been expanded from the GrabFood menu label 'The Alley Reindeer Plushie Deer Papa' to 'The Alley Reindeer Plushie Deer Papa Brown Sugar Milk Tea' to provide a more canonical and descriptive English identity. 19. Price SGD 30.80 as listed on GrabFood Singapore (The Alley - Funan); this likely reflects a bundle or set price including the plushie toy component, not solely the beverage. 20. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 requirements.
